forecasts.reasoning: Alassane Ouattara has strategically delayed announcing his candidacy, keeping the nation 'guessing' as of early 2025. In the previous election cycle (2020), he officially announced his run in early August for an October election. Côte d'Ivoire's electoral rules generally require candidate submissions 60 days prior to the election (late August for the October 25, 2025 vote). As the unchallenged 'natural candidate' of the ruling RHDP party with no pressing internal deadline to declare, Ouattara has a strong incentive to wait until summer, likely July or August (possibly coinciding with Independence Day on August 7), to officially declare. While an announcement in May is possible—perhaps around the May 6 anniversary of his assumption of power—it is substantially more likely he continues his pattern of delaying until the 2-3 months leading up to the election.
